ddls:
CREATE TABLE "nodes" (
  "node_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each node */,
  "node_name" TEXT /* Name of the node */,
  "node_description" TEXT /* Description of the node */,
  "node_type" TEXT /* Type of the node (e.g., master, worker, coordinator) */,
  "node_status" TEXT /* Current status of the node (e.g., active, maintenance, offline) */,
  "location" TEXT /* Physical or logical location of the node */,
  "last_heartbeat" TEXT /* Last time the node sent a heartbeat signal */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("node_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "metrics" (
  "metric_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each metric */,
  "node_id" INTEGER /* ID of the node that collected the metric */,
  "secs_elapsed" INTEGER /* Number of seconds elapsed since the start of the interval */,
  "interval_qps" INTEGER /* Query throughput per second */,
  "total_mem_size" INTEGER /* Total memory size used */,
  "l0_files" INTEGER /* Number of L0 files */,
  "pending_bytes" INTEGER /* Number of pending bytes */,
  "collection_time" TEXT /* Time when the metric was collected */,
  "cpu_usage" REAL /* CPU usage percentage */,
  "gpu_usage" REAL /* GPU usage percentage */,
  "disk_usage" REAL /* Disk usage percentage */,
  "network_in" INTEGER /* Incoming network traffic in bytes */,
  "network_out" INTEGER /* Outgoing network traffic in bytes */,
  "error_count" INTEGER /* Number of errors during the interval */,
  "query_latency" INTEGER /* Average query latency in milliseconds */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("metric_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_metrics_node_id FOREIGN KEY ("node_id") REFERENCES nodes ("node_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "alerts" (
  "alert_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each alert */,
  "node_id" INTEGER /* ID of the node that triggered the alert */,
  "metric_id" INTEGER /* ID of the metric that triggered the alert */,
  "alert_type" TEXT /* Type of alert (e.g., performance, resource usage) */,
  "alert_message" TEXT /* Message describing the alert */,
  "alert_time" TEXT /* Time when the alert was triggered */,
  "alert_severity" TEXT /* Severity of the alert (e.g., low, medium, high, critical) */,
  "resolved" INTEGER /* Indicates whether the alert has been resolved */,
  "resolution_time" TEXT /* Time when the alert was resolved */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("alert_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_alerts_metric_id FOREIGN KEY ("metric_id") REFERENCES metrics ("metric_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_alerts_node_id FOREIGN KEY ("node_id") REFERENCES nodes ("node_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "alert_config" (
  "config_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each configuration */,
  "node_id" INTEGER /* ID of the node that the configuration applies to */,
  "metric_name" TEXT /* Name of the metric being monitored */,
  "threshold_value" INTEGER /* Threshold value for the metric */,
  "alert_type" TEXT /* Type of alert (e.g., performance, resource usage) */,
  "notification_channel" TEXT /* Notification channel (e.g., email, SMS, messaging app) */,
  "alert_severity" TEXT /* Severity of the alert (e.g., low, medium, high, critical) */,
  "threshold_operator" TEXT /* Threshold operator (e.g., >, <, =) */,
  "alert_duration" INTEGER /* Duration for which the metric must exceed the threshold before an alert is triggered */,
  "escalation_level" TEXT /* Level of escalation for the alert (e.g., level 1, level 2) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("config_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_alert_config_node_id FOREIGN KEY ("node_id") REFERENCES nodes ("node_id")
)
